title: PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS - 211info
text: PORTLAND GENERAL ELECTRIC PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means PGE will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. 121 SW Salmon Street Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 544-1795 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 228-6322 Main phone (800) 542-8818 Toll-free Customer Service Main phone (800) 544-1795 Outages and Emergencies Alternate (503) 464-7777 Power Outages in Portland INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Columbia, Marion, Multnomah, Polk, Washington, and Yamhill Counties Services: Electric Service Connection/Repair * Disaster/Emergency Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: UTILITIES - INCOME-QUALIFIED BILL DISCOUNT PROGRAM UTILITIES - PORTLAND GENERAL ELECTRIC If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
